_meta.description: Test data aliases for @td() references in suite CSVs. Syntax: @td(ALIAS.field) or @td(file, filter, column)
_meta.changelog_1_5_6: Added PROD_FULL_FIELD_COVERAGE inline alias (id=0b6297e8-d100-4419-ac54-8bf240777271 / SKU ALCE0128 / Animal Crossing). Pins CAT-GQL-113 (regression/suites/Backend/graphql/050a-graphql-xcatalog.csv) to a known-good product with populated CatalogSeoInfo row, dropping the non-deterministic list_p first-product capture. Reason: storefront default product order shifts between runs, so items[0] could be a product without CatalogSeoInfo row → CAT-GQL-113 would flake into FAIL via the xAPI 3.1008.0 SeoInfo regression (tracked separately by BUG-xAPI-SeoInfo-NonNull-Resolver-NoData). The no-SeoInfo branch is now covered by the bug report; CAT-GQL-113 is now a clean schema-coverage gate.
_meta.changelog_1_5_5: Added CFG_017_SECTIONS inline alias (Text section 2cc0f57e-5654-4396-8795-66fe6e226ad1, config b7e534c2-c47f-4c22-ac9e-5c094f1b7f0d — verified live via productConfiguration on vcst-qa 2026-05-20). Refactored 6 references in regression/suites/Backend/graphql/050b3-graphql-xcart-lifecycle.csv from @td(CFG_RING.section_text_id) (unresolvable — CSV-backed CFG_RING never had that field) to @td(CFG_017_SECTIONS.Text). Matches the established CFG_022_SECTIONS / CFG_023_SECTIONS pattern. 050b3 validator now [OK] 19/19 resolved (was 13/19).
_meta.changelog_1_5_4: Repointed BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY (test-data/products/standard.csv STD-001) from SKU ALCOE9535 / id 06b6bc60-5553-4eca-b505-be679dda4013 to SKU ALCE0128 / id 0b6297e8-d100-4419-ac54-8bf240777271 (Animal Crossing New Horizons, $200, brand=Nintendo, category=Game Deals). Verified live on vcst-qa 2026-05-20: minQuantity=1, maxQuantity=0, packSize=1, isBuyable=true, availableQuantity=568, hasVariations=false. BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY contract preserved (addItem qty=1 succeeds without PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY rejection). Slug now stored as the full xAPI slug 'accessories/aliexpress/consumer-electronics/game-deals/animal-crossing-new-orizons-nintendo-ALCE0128'.
_meta.changelog_1_5_3: Phase 2 CFG reseed: CFG-013 Laptop (parent dd56d770, 2-section P!P!), CFG-017 Ring (parent 750d36bd, 1-section T! maxLength=30), CFG-019 Giftbox (parent 628fddc9, 1-section T? maxLength=100), CFG-020 Phone Case (parent 5792871f, 3-section P!P?T?), CFG-021 Custom Bike (parent 0b42a3eb, 3-section P!P!P?). Total 5 new parents + 14 new child option products. Configurations verified via productConfiguration xAPI. Storefront-visible. CFG_LAPTOP/CFG_RING/CFG_GIFTBOX aliases flipped from needs-reseed to reseeded-20260518-phase2. Phase 3 (conditional cascades CFG-022..029 with dependsOnSectionId chains) still pending — script needs dependsOnSectionId wiring + child product references. Phase 1+2 reseed total: 10 of 24 missing CFGs now alive.
_meta.changelog_1_5_1: Added VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B inline alias (id=fc596540864a41bf8ab78734ee7353a3) — single source of truth for storefront B2B virtual catalog root. Updated BOPIS testProductCatalogId from stale 9238c387… to fc596540864a41bf8ab78734ee7353a3. Refactored regression/suites/Backend/graphql/050a-graphql-xcatalog.csv: 10 hardcoded 9238c387… occurrences → @td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id). 35 occurrences of fc596540… in other Backend/graphql/050* suites still hardcoded but functionally correct — will be refactored to @td() in a follow-up PR.
_meta.changelog_1_3_0: Added 14 *_VIRTOSTART aliases for B2B entities seeded on virtostart-demo-admin (4 orgs / 10 users) — backed by b2b/organizations-virtostart.csv and b2b/users-virtostart.csv. vcst-qa aliases unchanged.
_meta.changelog_1_4_0: Added IMPERSONATE_TARGET (→ b2b/users.csv USR-003 Mike Torres, AcmeCorp viewer) and OTHER_ORG_USER (→ b2b/users.csv USR-007 David Kim, TechFlow buyer) for VCST-4905 Login-on-behalf form/silent flow cases (AUTH-027..AUTH-081 in suite 031). Both point to existing seeded platform users — no new seeding needed.
_meta.changelog_1_4_1: VCST-4905 scenario correction: repointed IMPERSONATE_TARGET to USR-007 David Kim (was Mike Torres); added SUPPORT_AGENT → USR-001 John Mitchell (ACME admin) as the support persona who fills the Security Verification Form. Canonical happy-path: John (support, own credentials) → impersonates David (target, different org). PRECONDITION: The role assigned to SUPPORT_AGENT must include StorefrontPermissions.CanImpersonate for the impersonate-step to succeed. Permissions live on Roles (Admin SPA > Security > Roles > [role] > Permissions), NOT directly on users. As of 2026-05-11 verification: role assigned to USR-001 does not include this permission — POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate returns 403. Fix: add CanImpersonate to the existing role or assign a role that includes it.
_meta.changelog_1_4_2: Fixed OTHER_ORG_USER alias data bug — was pointing to USR-007 (same user as IMPERSONATE_TARGET, both ORG-002); now points to USR-008 Carlos Rodriguez (ORG-003 BuildRight). Unblocks IMP-018 cross-org impersonation rejection test in suite 082-auth-impersonation.csv.
_meta.changelog_1_4_3: Added inline AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE + AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER aliases for IMP-033/034/038 in suite 082-auth-impersonation.csv. Inline (no CSV row) because these are ephemeral entities created+destroyed by the tests themselves — names are stable contracts, not env-resolved data.
_meta.changelog_1_4_4: Added IMPERSONATE_TARGET_MANY_ORGS (USR-020), IMPERSONATE_TARGET_BLOCKED (USR-021), IMPERSONATE_TARGET_INVITED (USR-022) for IMP-048 and IMP-049 in suite 082-auth-impersonation.csv. All three point to b2b/users.csv rows that do not yet exist — FIXTURE-SEEDING REQUIRED before first run. See _comment on each alias for provisioning instructions.
_meta.changelog_1_4_5: Seeded IMPERSONATE_TARGET_MANY_ORGS (USR-020 — Password1!, 11 AGENT-TEST orgs), IMPERSONATE_TARGET_BLOCKED (USR-021 — Locked), IMPERSONATE_TARGET_INVITED (USR-022 — EmailUnconfirmed) on vcst-qa via scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs. Added 11 supporting AGENT-TEST orgs (ORG-009..ORG-019) to b2b/organizations.csv; added 3 contacts (CON-020..CON-022) to b2b/contacts.csv; added org_count column to b2b/users.csv. Data-drift note: ORG-001 (AcmeCorp eba8b270-...) and ORG-004 (AcmeWest a1e98b8e-...) platform_ids in organizations.csv are STALE on vcst-qa as of 2026-05-14 — search returns 0 results; USR-001..USR-004 contacts now point to TechFlow's id (6fb516c1-...). USR-020 therefore uses 11 NEW AGENT-TEST orgs rather than mixing 4 base + 7 new. USR-021/022 are assigned to ORG-002 (TechFlow), not ORG-001. Observed Locked enum on platform: lockoutEnd='9999-12-31T23:59:59.9999999+00:00' + lockoutEnabled=true + /api/platform/security/users/{id}/locked returns {locked:true}; status field remains null (the platform persists Locked state via Identity Framework lockoutEnd, not via UserState/status enum). Observed Invited enum: emailConfirmed=false + lockoutEnabled=true + status=null (the platform has no dedicated 'Invited' UserState enum — invitation is signaled purely by emailConfirmed=false + sendVerificationEmail trigger).
_meta.changelog_1_4_6: Locked IMP-049 VERIFY assertions on first run (2026-05-14, qa-testing-expert, Firefox MCP, vcst-qa, platform 3.1026.0, theme vc-theme-b2b-vue-2.49.0-pr-2280-8069-80690ef2). Observed contracts — Blocked: HTTP 200 with valid Bearer token (sub=blocked, vc_operator_user_id=operator); storefront frontend wipes auth and redirects to /blocked; operator session is DESTROYED as a side effect. Invited: HTTP 200 with valid Bearer token (sub=invited, vc_operator_user_id=operator); silent success, lands on Home, operator chip + 'Back to {operator}' revert button visible in header. SECURITY GAP confirmed via REST cross-check: platform /connect/token grant_type=impersonate has NO status-based gate — direct REST callers with platform:security:loginOnBehalf permission can mint a usable 30-min Bearer for Locked OR Invited targets, bypassing the storefront UI's /blocked enforcement entirely. Full contract evidence at tests/Sprint-current/VCST-4906/evidence/imp-049/ and report at tests/Sprint-current/VCST-4906/test-execution-report-imp-049.md.
_meta.changelog_1_5_0: Post-restore (2026-05-15) status flags applied to CFG_* aliases. Affected: 28 CFG entries got _status field (shape-drift / weak-match / needs-reseed / new-survivor / unchanged). Added 3 new aliases for restored-env survivors (CFG_BED c7205a92…, CFG_BIKE_TEXT 05a8b8a5…, CFG_WEDDING_CAKE_VINTAGE c94d730a…) — these are inline (no CSV row) since they were not in the original test data. Catalog root reverted to fc596540864a41bf8ab78734ee7353a3 (the 2026-04-30 migration to 9238c387… rolled back by restore). The BOPIS testProductCatalogId reference to 9238c387… is now STALE and should be updated to fc596540… in a follow-up. 5 CFG products fully alive with matching GUIDs (Hat 38dbe95c, T-shirt 50529b79, Vintage Hoodie 4b1de1b0, Bike with options f16d3e8f, Off-Road Bike 958d0762) — 4 with shape drift (sections differ from CSV spec). 24 CFG products gone — needs-reseed flag indicates which need a fresh seed PR (Laptop, Ring, Giftbox, conditional cascades CFG-022..029). Full mapping rationale: tests/reseed-2026-05-18/cfg-alias-mapping-proposal.md.
_meta.changelog_1_5_2: Phase 1 CFG reseed complete: CFG-012/014/015/016/018 now have working products in vcst-qa with new GUIDs (da3f85bb, ae791be7, 3b0052b9, 37d2f9ae, 03f6007c respectively). All have configurations with 1 Product-type section + 2-4 options. Parent products linked into VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B and storefront-visible (isAvailable=true, isBuyable=true). Child option products have inventory in ffc-acme-eur-berlin-de with status=Enabled. Aliases that reference these 5 CFGs now resolve correctly. Pricelist SEED-20260518-Configurables-USD assigned to B2B-store. Aliases touched: . Phase 2 (multi-section CFG-006/013/020/021) and Phase 3 (conditional cascades CFG-022..029) deferred to follow-up PR.

PROD_FULL_FIELD_COVERAGE.name: Animal Crossing New Horizons Nintendo Switch Game Deals
PROD_FULL_FIELD_COVERAGE._notes: Schema-coverage fixture for CAT-GQL-113 (xCatalog — Product Full-Field Schema Coverage). MUST be a product with a populated CatalogSeoInfo row so that seoInfo.{id,objectId,objectType} resolve. Animal Crossing (ALCE0128 / 0b6297e8-…) was verified 2026-05-20 to have SeoInfo row 49c1b977-6cc6-413f-9022-1ffae7328cc5 → semanticUrl 'animal-crossing-new-orizons-nintendo-ALCE0128'. Previously CAT-GQL-113 picked products.items.0 which was non-deterministic (storefront default order shifts; first product often had no SeoInfo → bug BUG-xAPI-SeoInfo-NonNull-Resolver-NoData surfaced). Pinning here makes the test a deterministic schema-coverage gate; the no-SeoInfo regression is now covered by the dedicated bug report, not this test.

OTHER_ORG_USER._comment: Cross-org user — USR-008 Carlos Rodriguez (BuildRight, ORG-003). Genuinely different org from both SUPPORT_AGENT (ORG-001) and IMPERSONATE_TARGET (ORG-002). Used by IMP-018 cross-org impersonation rejection test. Fixed 2026-05-12: was previously pointing to USR-007 (same as IMPERSONATE_TARGET) — see VCST-4905 alias bug.

BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.notes: B2B parent product buyable at qty=1, no max cap (max_qty=0 means unlimited). Verified 2026-05-20: SKU ALCE0128 Animal Crossing New Horizons / GUID 0b6297e8-d100-4419-ac54-8bf240777271. minQuantity=1, maxQuantity=0, packSize=1, hasVariations=false, isBuyable=true, availableQuantity=568. For GraphQL xCart suites (050b1, 050k) that need addItem(qty:1) to succeed without PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY rejection. Predecessors: Canvas Pencil Case ALCOE9535 (06b6bc60 / b3f5bd0c, replaced 2026-05-20 by user request); original setup_prod filter that returned parent 8e0eaa82 with min_qty=10 caused silent rejection (REG-2026-04-29-0929 / BUG-050b1-001).

USER_MULTI_ORG.notes: Template only — NOT seeded. Member of 2+ B2B orgs (ORG-001 AcmeCorp + ORG-002 TechFlow) simultaneously. Triggers org-selector UI in cart. Unblocks CART-052 CART-053. Env vars: MULTI_ORG_USER_EMAIL, MULTI_ORG_USER_PASSWORD.

USER_EUR.notes: Reuses existing USR-011 (Hans Mueller — EU admin with multi-language + EUR currency). Unblocks CART-059. Env vars: EUR_USER_EMAIL, EUR_USER_PASSWORD.

TECHFLOW_ORG_ADDRESSES.notes: VCST-4710 OQ-5 seed 2026-04-23. 20 new addresses (10 US, 5 CA, 5 UK) plus 2 pre-existing. Unblocks SF-004/006/007/009/010/012 (list/search/facet/sort) and GQ-003/004/005 (currentOrganizationAddresses). Full per-address IDs in test-data/addresses/_seed-results-techflow-20260423.json. Known caveat: platform CustomerAddress schema has no isFavorite field — storefront MemberAddressType returns isFavorite=false for all 20 after seed; toggle favorite via storefront UI if needed.

TECHFLOW_ADDR_CA_TORONTO.notes: VCST-4710 seed — primary CA/ON address fixture for multi-country facet tests.

TECHFLOW_ADDR_UK_LONDON.notes: VCST-4710 seed — default org address (isDefault=true). Primary UK/ENG fixture for GB country-facet tests.

ADDR_NO_MATCH_TERM.notes: VCST-4710 SA-009 EP empty-results search term for address-popup search field. Guaranteed not to match any seeded address; drives the no-results empty state.

Used for personal-account popup schema tests (SA-024/025/026/029 in suite 081).

Counts: USA=12, CAN=5, GBR=5, total=22. Per project_address_state_facet.md, state/province facet only renders for USA/CAN — GBR addresses must hide it. Multi-match needles guarantee >1 row to validate filter behavior. Re-snapshot to a NEW filename if address set changes; do NOT mutate the 20260423 file.

COUPONS.freeShippingCode_value: Free shipping ($999 off shipping cost), expires 2026-12-31
COUPONS.expiredCode_value: Coupon on inactive promotion — must be rejected
COUPONS.usedUpCode_value: Pre-used coupon at total limit — second use rejected. PRECONDITION: requires one prior redemption by USER_EMAIL before test runs.
COUPONS.invalidCode_value: String only — never seeded; expected 'invalid coupon' rejection
COUPONS.bestRewardActive_source: project_promotion_engine.md — QA store uses BestRewardPromotionPolicy (default for B2B-store). When applying multiple eligible promotions, the engine selects the single best discount; coupon-backed rewards win ties over automatic ones.
COUPONS.notes: VCST-4896 GAP-03 coupons-sidebar fixtures. PROVISIONED — all five seeded codes already exist in QA env via promotions/coupons.csv (no new seeding done). usedUpCode requires 1-time redemption pre-step before each test run. invalidCode is a literal random string (do NOT seed). bestRewardActive=true means tests must expect winner-take-all reward selection, not stacking. Tag any new admin-side coupons created during test runs with prefix 'AGENT-TEST-CPN-' for /qa-seed-data teardown.

BOPIS.lastPageIndex_convention: 1-based; position N (1..totalCount). For 0-based array index use lastPageIndex - 1 = 101.
BOPIS.testProductName: Dry Erase Blank Playing Card Reusable Blank Cards Board Game Cards Study Guide & Note Cards Word or Math Training Teaching Aids
BOPIS.testProduct_source_query: products(storeId:"B2B-store", cultureName:"en-US", first:5) on FRONT_URL/graphql — first item with availabilityData.isAvailable=true and price.actual.amount>0
BOPIS.testProduct_bopis_eligibility: RUNTIME_VERIFY — store-level pickup is enabled (102 indexed locations for B2B-store); per-product BOPIS flag was not exposed by storefront xAPI products() at capture time. If checkout shows no pickup option for ALCOE2300, swap to another in-stock product or seed a known-BOPIS SKU before BOPIS-092/093/080 run.
BOPIS.admin_endpoint: POST {BACK_URL}/api/shipping/pickup-locations/search (returns 157 cross-store; storefront filter narrows to 102 for B2B-store)
BOPIS.cursor_convention: after=N is exclusive — pass after='49' to start at position 50, after='50' to start at position 51.
BOPIS.notes: VCST-4707 / Sprint 26-08 GAP-05 (BOPIS-092/093/080). PROVISIONED — no seeding required; QA already has 102 indexed pickup locations for B2B-store (well above 51 threshold). locationCount/lastPageIndex sourced from live storefront xAPI pickupLocations query. pageSize=50 verified by paging first=50 then after='50'. indexedSearchEnabled=true confirmed via GET /api/shipping/pickup-locations/indexedSearchEnabled (per VCST-4631 flag). Position-50/51 ids are stable as long as the underlying dataset and default sort don't change — re-snapshot to a NEW filename if the count or ordering shifts (do NOT mutate the 20260430 file). Existing BOPIS_DOWNTOWN/MALL/BEACH aliases reference pickup-locations.csv which is the legacy CSV-driven seed pool (BOPIS-LOC-001..050) and is NOT directly tied to this live-environment snapshot. testSku/testProductId added 2026-04-30 for BOPIS-092/093/080 'add product to cart before testing pickup pre-selection' steps — ALCOE2300 chosen because it was the first storefront-visible product with isAvailable=true and a positive price; B2B virtual catalog id at capture was 9238c387-d779-40cb-b27d-5496a670a924 (NOT the fc596540... id referenced by older memory notes — that filter returned 0 products on this date). BOPIS eligibility for this specific SKU was not directly confirmed via xAPI; treat as RUNTIME_VERIFY and swap if needed.

IMPERSONATION_ADMIN.notes: VCST-4725 GAP-09 Page Builder preview impersonation. PROVISIONED 2026-04-30. Created via POST /api/platform/security/users/create with isAdministrator=true. AGENT-TEST- prefixed (cleanup via /qa-seed-data teardown). Credentials live in .env (IMPERSONATION_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD) per feedback_agents_read_env_creds — NEVER inline plaintext. CAVEAT: live Platform permissions registry has 0 matches for /impersonat/i across 301 perms — exact permission key for Page Builder impersonation could not be resolved within the 5-minute investigation budget. Falling back to Administrator role (isAdministrator=true) which grants the superset. If a granular permission key is later identified (likely shipped by a CMS / Page Builder / x-portal module), update this alias and downgrade the role. Target customer reuses TECHFLOW_BUYER (USR-007) — no new customer created; orgId is the existing seeded TechFlow org.

The variationParentUrl field is redundant: 8 conditional-section CFG products (CFG-022..029) already exist as full aliases in this registry, each with a 'url' field resolved from products/configurable-products.csv. Test cases should reference one of those existing aliases (e.g., @td(CFG_TEXT_DRIVEN_COND.url)) instead of a separate ADDRESS_SEARCH-style needle. ADMIN_DEFAULT.auth_verified_via: POST {BACK_URL}/connect/token grant_type=password username=$ADMIN password=$ADMIN_PASSWORD scope=offline_access — returned access_token (length 1582) plus refresh_token; GET /api/platform/security/currentuser returned isAdministrator:true id:1eb2fa8ac6574541afdb525833dadb46
ADMIN_DEFAULT.notes: Sprint 26-08 review — provisioned 2026-04-30 to satisfy [PRE:SIGNIN_AS:ADMIN_DEFAULT] in ORD-091..095 (017-orders-admin-management.csv) and CFG-E2E-073-COND (072b-configurable-products-e2e.csv). Resolves to the platform built-in admin user. Per feedback_agents_read_env_creds, the alias references env vars (ADMIN, ADMIN_PASSWORD) — runners must read .env at runtime; NEVER inline plaintext. The semantic 'roles' array reports ['Administrator'] because isAdministrator=true grants the Administrator superset; the 'explicit_role_assignments' field captures the additional non-superset roles attached to this user record (Theme settings changer, SQL queries) for traceability. userId is the GUID-without-hyphens form returned by /api/platform/security/users/admin and /api/platform/security/currentuser — preserve as-is, it is the canonical platform Identity user id.

validPdf.generator: scripts/inline node — handcrafted minimal PDF/1.4 (Catalog, Pages, single Page with Helvetica text 'CFG-PDP-048 test fixture - 1 page')
validPdf._purpose: CFG-PDP-048 happy-path File section upload. Stays well under @td(CFG_FILE_HOODIE.maxFileSizeMb)=9.5 MB and exercises a real PDF MIME type (not octet-stream). Single-page so the configuration preview / receipt rendering paths can render predictably. Re-generate (do NOT mutate this file in place) if the underlying validator changes (e.g., PDF/A required, signature required); update sha256 + sizeBytes if regenerated.

CFG_TEST_FILES._status_note: File-upload fixture aliases — no product GUID dependency. Unaffected by restore.

CFG_TEXT_INPUTS.notes: Sprint 26-08 review — literal-value alias for configurable Text section Custom-input tests (CFG-PDP-050-COND in 072-configurable-products-ui.csv). 'customValue' is a deterministic alphanumeric+hyphen string of length 22, safely under the smallest known Text section maxLength in the registry (CFG_RING.maxLength=30) so the same value works whether the underlying product is CFG_RING (max 30), CFG_GIFTBOX (max 100), CFG_TEXT_DRIVEN_COND, or any other Text-section product. No special chars (no quotes/backslashes) so it survives JSON/CSV/GraphQL escaping without modification. 'maxLength' is an echo for tests that want to assert against the cap; populated as 30 to match the strictest registered Text section. 'presetOptionId' is null because no preset is required for the Custom-input cascade test (CFG-PDP-050-COND types into the Custom input, it does not select a preset)—populate per-product if a future preset-aware test needs it. Per project_configurable_text_section_validation.md, maxLength applies to Custom input only, so this value is sized for Custom-input scenarios. NOT a discovery alias — no API call needed; safe to consume directly via @td(CFG_TEXT_INPUTS.customValue).
CFG_TEXT_INPUTS._status_note: Text-input fixture aliases — no product GUID dependency. Unaffected by restore.

CFG_VARIATION_CAKE.captured_via: products(storeId:B2B-store, query:THT-36341598) + productConfiguration + product(id, storeId).variations + storefront PDP screenshot live on vcst-qa 2026-05-07
CFG_VARIATION_CAKE._featureStatus: Variation-section rendering is NOT yet implemented on the storefront frontend (vc-frontend). Backend xAPI exposes the Variation-type configurationSection, but it returns empty options[]. The storefront PDP renders product.variations as a SEPARATE 'PRODUCT VARIATIONS' panel below the configurator (each variation is a standalone purchase line) — this is a different code path from configurable Variation sections. Do NOT write end-to-end test coverage for Variation-section selection mutations until both: (a) backend wires variation options into the configurationSection metadata, AND (b) frontend implements section rendering. Until then, only CFG-GQL-033 (xAPI structural check — section exists with type=Variation, options[] currently empty) is meaningful. CFG-GQL-034..036 (preview/upsert/flip) were drafted then removed 2026-05-07 as premature.
CFG_VARIATION_CAKE.notes: Reference product for future Variation-section coverage. PR #114 Variation contract (when implemented): ConfigurationSectionKeyInput uses {sectionId, type:'Variation', option:{productId}} — same shape as Product type. Once feature ships, expected option product ids will be 'c56a12c0-...' ($120) and '116a85f5-...' ($200) from productVariations[].
CFG_VARIATION_CAKE._status_note: Original referenced Variation-section behavior. CFG-022 family Variation sections all gone. Vintage Wedding cake has 5 Product + 1 Text + 1 File sections — no Variation type.

CFG_REQUIRED_FILE_HOODIE.productName: Hoodie Base product with only File req
CFG_REQUIRED_FILE_HOODIE.captured_via: products(query:OSW-31158373) + productConfiguration + addItem live verification on https://vcst-qa.govirto.com/graphql 2026-05-06
CFG_REQUIRED_FILE_HOODIE._priceDiscrepancyFinding: Live verification 2026-05-06: xAPI products() returns list=250 actual=250, BUT addItem-committed lineItem.listPrice = 300 (a $50 / 20% gap). Either (a) the configured-product reprice path uses a different price source than catalog products() — potential P1 finding to investigate separately, OR (b) the BA analysis from 2026-03-24 (sale list $300 / sale $250 / -17%) is the historical state and the product's catalog price has since been raised back to $300 with no active sale. Tests should NOT assert exact sale price until the source-of-truth for configured-product listPrice is confirmed.
CFG_REQUIRED_FILE_HOODIE.notes: REQUIRED File section variant of CFG_FILE_HOODIE — single section, required=true, no None option. Use for: (a) REQUIRED-File deselect contract test (parallel to CFG-GQL-027/028 for Product/Text); (b) source-of-truth investigation for configured-product listPrice (xAPI vs lineItem reprice path). CFG_FILE_HOODIE (CFG-004) has OPTIONAL file; this product is the required-file counterpart for fuller PR #114 coverage.
CFG_REQUIRED_FILE_HOODIE._status_note: CFG-005 Hoodie File-only required. Survivor candidates: Tramadol (4975c7d6…) or Revlimid (acfaad21…) — both [F!] single-section. Repoint via test-case update.

Per project_configurable_text_section_validation.md, maxLength applies to Custom input only — preset 'Try add text more than 5' is 25 chars and is allowed because it's a preset.

Use for: (a) Optional Product section selection toggle (different from CFG_LAPTOP which has only required Product); (b) Required Text deselect with tight maxLen (CFG_RING/CFG-017 has maxLen=30 — INN-69077289 stresses the lower bound at maxLen=5).

CFG_WEDDING_CAKE_CONDITIONS.notes: Wedding cake with conditional 5-section graph: Base (req root) → Creme (opt) → Message (opt) → [Custom text required (req Text, maxLen=50), Image (opt File)]. The richest CFG product in QA — exercises ALL 4 section types (Product/Text/File) AND conditional dependencies AND mixed-required semantics. Use for: (a) mixed-type selection toggle in single batch; (b) conditional cascade interaction with selectedForCheckout flag; (c) parent deselect → does it cascade-disable child configurationItems? (open contract question — PR #114 doesn't specify). Distinct from CFG_WEDDING_CAKE_CONDITIONAL (CFG-023, agent-test-prefixed seed) — YOC-85609878 is the persistent QA fixture.

UPLOAD_FIXTURES._purpose: Curated runtime upload fixtures for File-section selection-mutation tests (CFG-GQL-029 in 050i-graphql-configurations.csv) and storefront file-upload regressions (072-series). Runner uploads the file via REST (POST {BACK_URL}/api/platform/assets/upload OR storefront file-upload mutation) at test time and captures the returned URL into {{UPLOADED_FILE_URL}}. All listed files are <9.5MB so they pass @td(CFG_FILE_HOODIE.maxFileSizeMb) validation. Re-introspect sizeBytes after any directory edit.

primary._purpose: Default File-section upload fixture — small PNG, deterministic content. Used by CFG-GQL-029.

smallPdf._purpose: PDF variant for File-section MIME-discrimination tests.

smallXlsx._purpose: Excel variant for File-section MIME variety; smallest practical document fixture.

jpg._purpose: JPEG variant; case-insensitive extension stress (JPg vs jpg).

svg._purpose: SVG variant; XML-based vector format for File-section parser stress.

uploadEndpoint.auth: Bearer (ORG_USER token from /connect/token; storefront login_to=B2B-store) — endpoint also responds 200 with structured error to anonymous POST, so probe-style validation works without token
uploadEndpoint.responseShape: JSON array; consume [0]. Fields: { succeeded: bool, errorCode, errorMessage, scope (e.g. 'product-configuration'), name, contentType, size, url (relative path — feed into fileUrls), publicUrl, ownerEntityId, ownerEntityType, id }
uploadEndpoint.verifiedVia: Live probe on https://vcst-qa.govirto.com/api/files/product-configuration — empty POST returned 200 + structured error shape confirming endpoint deployment and response contract.

UPLOAD_FIXTURES.notes: Runner upload contract: when a step references @td(UPLOAD_FIXTURES.<key>.path), the runner reads the file from disk, uploads via [REST-OP rest_upload] step (POST @td(UPLOAD_FIXTURES.uploadEndpoint.url) with multipart-form file=@<path>), and captures the returned response[0].url as {{UPLOADED_FILE_URL}}. Subsequent GraphQL steps consume {{UPLOADED_FILE_URL}} in fileUrls[]. If response[0].succeeded=false, the test is BLOCKED with the response[0].errorMessage. The endpoint is xCart-specific (scope='product-configuration'); do NOT use /api/platform/assets/upload — that's the platform-wide assets folder and does NOT register the file as a configurable-product asset.

SEARCH_QUERY.verified_via: POST {BACK_URL}/graphql products(storeId:"B2B-store", query:"ring", first:5) — totalCount=77 (well above the >=3 threshold in SRCH-NEW-055)
SEARCH_QUERY.notes: Sprint 26-08 review — generic search term that returns multiple hits on the QA storefront, used by SRCH-NEW-055 in 004-search-core.csv (mobile results page layout at 375px). 'ring' was chosen because (a) it was already named as the in-line fallback in SRCH-NEW-055 Steps, (b) it returned totalCount=77 in live xAPI verification on 2026-04-30 (against the active B2B virtual catalog root 9238c387-d779-40cb-b27d-5496a670a924 — see feedback_graphql_products_filter.md), and (c) it is short, lowercase, ASCII-only, so it round-trips through URL/query-string/CSV without escaping concerns. expectedMinHits=77 is a snapshot of the verification count, not a runtime assertion target — tests should still assert >=3 results (per SRCH-NEW-055 precondition) so the suite stays env-resilient if the catalog churns. Per feedback_env_resilience.md, do NOT promote this alias to assert the exact count of 77.

AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE._comment: Ephemeral test role created and destroyed by IMP-033/034/038 in suite 082-auth-impersonation.csv. Never seeded on a persistent environment. AGENT-TEST-* naming follows the /qa-seed-data teardown convention — any orphaned instances are removed by /qa-seed-data teardown.
AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.name: AGENT-TEST-imp-role
AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.description: Test role for impersonation regression — safe to delete
AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE.permission_label: Login on behalf of a customer

AGENT_TEST_IMP_CUSTOMER._comment: Ephemeral test customer user created and destroyed by IMP-034/038. REQUIRED provisioning when (re)creating via Admin SPA: (1) Security > Users > Add (Account type=Customer, Status=Approved, Password=Password1!, no roles) → Save. (2) On the user-DETAIL blade (NOT Add dialog), set Container store=B2B-store → Save again (without B2B-store the user cannot authenticate against the storefront). (3) Create an associated Contact record (Customers > Contacts > Add, link to this user account — required for B2B storefront flows). (4) Assign AGENT_TEST_IMP_ROLE. Password is the standard test password (Password1!) — see user_test_accounts memory.

IMPERSONATE_TARGET_MANY_ORGS._comment: SEEDED on vcst-qa 2026-05-14 (scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs). Target user is member of 11 AGENT-TEST Active orgs (ORG-009..ORG-019: BMW-Group, Bence-and-Family, Brand-Specials, Cypress-Company-Kft, Elena-Company, Fill-Fillips-Company, Graceland-Boots-Shoes, Hillcrest-Holdings, Ironwood-Industries, Juniper-Junction, Kingsbridge-Imports). Exercises org-switcher scroll+search behavior in IMP-048. Note: vcst-qa data drift on 2026-05-14 — original ORG-001 AcmeCorp + ORG-004 AcmeWest no longer exist in live DB, so this user uses 11 fresh AGENT-TEST orgs rather than mixing 4 base + 7 new. To re-seed (e.g. after DB refresh), run: node scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs (idempotent). Storefront verification: sign in with AGENT-TEST-imp-target-many-orgs-20260514@test-agent.com / Password1!, click org switcher in top header; should show 11 rows with scroll + searchable partial match.

IMPERSONATE_TARGET_BLOCKED._comment: SEEDED on vcst-qa 2026-05-14 (scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs). Target user whose platform account is Locked at the user level — distinct from ORG-006 (suspended org). Required by IMP-049 Part A. Observed platform encoding of 'Locked': lockoutEnd='9999-12-31T23:59:59.9999999+00:00' + lockoutEnabled=true; GET /api/platform/security/users/{id}/locked returns {"locked":true}; UserState/status remains null (the platform uses ASP.NET Core Identity's lockoutEnd field, NOT a separate enum). User belongs to ORG-002 (TechFlow) — the user-level Locked status is the variable under test, not org status. To re-seed (e.g. after DB refresh), run: node scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs (idempotent — re-applies lock via POST /api/platform/security/users/{id}/lock). LOCKED CONTRACT 2026-05-14 (IMP-049 Part A): POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate user_id=<this> returns HTTP 200 with a valid 30-min Bearer (sub=this, vc_operator_user_id=operator). The platform OAuth endpoint has NO status-based gate — Locked accounts CAN be impersonated at the API level. The storefront frontend detects the Locked status downstream, wipes its own auth state, and redirects to /blocked (heading 'Your account is blocked'). Operator session is destroyed in the process. Direct REST callers bypass the /blocked redirect entirely. See tests/Sprint-current/VCST-4906/evidence/imp-049/ for token response bodies + screenshots.

IMPERSONATE_TARGET_INVITED._comment: SEEDED on vcst-qa 2026-05-14 (scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs). Target user who was Invited (verification email sent) but never completed email verification. Observed platform encoding of 'Invited': emailConfirmed=false + sendVerificationEmail has been issued; the VC platform has NO dedicated 'Invited' UserState enum — invitation state is signaled purely by emailConfirmed=false. status/userState fields remain null. User belongs to ORG-002 (TechFlow). The platform_id resolves to a valid user record so /account/impersonate/{userId} receives a real target with verification pending. To re-seed (e.g. after DB refresh), run: node scripts/seed-impersonation-targets.mjs (idempotent — re-issues sendVerificationEmail). The dummy password Password1! is set but the user should be treated as not having a usable password since email verification gates first sign-in. LOCKED CONTRACT 2026-05-14 (IMP-049 Part B): POST /connect/token grant_type=impersonate user_id=<this> returns HTTP 200 with a valid 30-min Bearer (sub=this, vc_operator_user_id=operator). emailConfirmed=false does NOT gate the impersonate grant. Impersonation succeeds silently — storefront lands on Home, header chrome shows operator chip + 'Back to {operator}' revert button. Operator can fully act as the Invited user. May or may not be by design depending on the desired support workflow. See tests/Sprint-current/VCST-4906/evidence/imp-049/ for token response bodies + screenshots.

VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.name: B2B-mixed (virtual catalog bound to B2B-store)
VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B._notes: Active B2B virtual catalog root for storefront product queries. Verified 2026-05-18 via store(storeId:"B2B-store") { catalogId }. The 2026-04-30 migration to 9238c387-d779-40cb-b27d-5496a670a924 was rolled back by the 2026-05-15 catalog restore. Use @td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id) inside GraphQL filter strings: filter: "category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id) price.USD:(0 TO)". If B2B-store rebinds again, only this one value needs updating.
